faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Gravesend home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cotton garments, webbing in closet corners, silken tubes or cases on fabrics, and small beige or gold-colored moths flying around closets or bedrooms. You may also notice larvae that look like small white caterpillars on clothing or carpet edges.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Gravesend?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Gravesend typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your property, and the number of treatment areas required. Our technicians provide free inspections and detailed quotes based on your specific situation.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically shows results within days as adult moths are eliminated, but complete eradication requires 4-6 weeks to address all life stages including eggs and larvae. Most Gravesend homes require 2-3 follow-up treatments spaced 2-3 weeks apart to ensure all moths are eliminated and prevent re-infestation.

faqs.question: Can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes! We provide comprehensive prevention guidance including regular vacuuming of closets and carpets, proper storage of woolens in sealed containers, periodic inspection of stored fabrics, and maintaining low humidity levels. We also offer ongoing monitoring services to catch any new moth activity early and protect your Gravesend home year-round.
